titleInfo.title: Sichuan Yingshan Xian Taipeng Shan moya tike diaocha jianbao
四川營山縣太蓬山摩崖題刻調查簡報
A Brief Report on the Survey of the Rock Inscriptions on Mount Taipeng in Yingshan County, Sichuan

name.namePart: Xihua shifan daxue lishi wenhua xueyuan
西華師範大學歷史文化學院
Chongqing Gongshang daxue jisuanji kexue yu xinxi gongcheng xueyuan
重慶工商大學計算機科學與信息工程學院

mods.abstract: This article mentions surveys undertaken in 1954, in the 1980ies, and in July 2008 by the Institute of Culture and History of the China West Normal University 西華師範大學歷史文化學院 and the Bureau for Cultural Relics of Yingshan County 營山縣文物管理所. The article introduces part of the 108 inscriptions documented for Mount Taipeng. 3 are illegible; 9 from the Tang, 1 from Five Dynasties, 31 from the Song, 4 from the Yuan, 7 from the Ming, 14 from the Qing, 3 from the Republic, 42 cannot be classified. They are listed according to genre. First, votive inscriptions; second, Buddhist sutra texts; third, spells; fourth, poems; and finally, inscriptions written in ink (p. 14) The authors explain that the text is first written with ink on paper. Then the paper sheets are glued to the rock. Over time, the paper decays, but the ink stains the rock. The authors seem to have transcribed as many texts as possible, given the weathered condition of the inscriptions.
